primary_field: Provisional Application 14 — accumulating innovations since Prov 13 filed 2026-04-12

adjacents.field: patent strategy + Cooperative Defensive Patent Pledge (#2260)

filing_target: TBD — Harrity #1 or Mousilli #2, before Nov 26 2026 conversion deadline for Prov 1

observation: Scribe Prov14 opened. Primary field covers all innovations surfaced since Prov 13 filed 2026-04-12 (App 64/036,646, #2225-#2262, 37 innovations, 27 Crown Jewels). Witness at Level 2 for patent strategy and Pledge structure; Level 3 for Crown Jewel criteria; Level 4 for IP allocation.

Prov 14 has 5 innovations pre-inventoried at B110 K422: #2263 Triple-Redundant Verification Architecture (CJ, pre-tagged), #2264 Commons Licensing Dividend (CJ, pre-tagged), #2265 Commons Admission Protocol (CJ, pre-tagged), #2266 Opt-In Member Documentation with Benefits (CJ, recommend), #2267 Member-Generated Guide Corpus (CJ, recommend). Sub-totals: Chessboard 1 / Open Water 4.

B111-B116 A&A candidates not yet inventoried for Prov 14: (a) SP-21 Tidbit Scribe — verification-discipline ledger with auto-append schema. (b) Three Fates Router (Clotho-Lachesis-Atropos) — pipelined AI session-listener that categorizes traffic and invokes sleeping domain-Scribes. (c) Scribes Cathedral — domain-indexed working memory with triply-redundant witness, 6 levels expandable to 12. (d) Member-Owned Scribes Cathedral — consent-gated shareable Scribe tablets as membership product built on (c), feeding R9 personal preload. (e) Cost-Slasher Claim Ladder — empirically-defensible marketing framework (50%+ conservative / up to 95% aggressive) tied to cost-per-correct-answer metric. (f) Fingerprint-Based Incremental Index Reconciliation (K429) — mtime-diff staleness detection for multi-source indexes with async auto-reconcile in session-start hook. (g) Parallel Preload Multilingual Retrieval (Chapter 2 Mellon / K435) — lang-parameterized knowledge-base where parallel translations are sibling preloads, not replacements. Each needs A&A extraction before filing.

Founder insight (B116): Scribes Cathedral is ALSO a member-facing product — members get their own domain-indexed working memory across their workflows. Huge membership incentive especially for R9 users (Cathedrals amplify R9 accuracy on their personal preload). Sets up a new innovation candidate: Member-Owned Scribes Cathedral. Ecosystem extension: optional consent-gated Scribe-sharing within Guild/Tribe per #2260 Pledge.

K432 shipped by Knight — Pedestal Stake Phase 2 complete. 18/18 tests green. Two new patent-candidate patterns surfaced: (a) Pluggable Multi-Provider Adapter with Env-Var Switch (KYC stub/Middesk/Alloy + funding-portal stub/Republic/Wefunder, factory-in-index pattern, same interface contract); (b) Two-Track Context Badge UX (visual separation between Liana Banyan Member context and Upekrithen Stakeholder context in same app, preserving two-track separation at presentation layer). Both should be A&A-evaluated for Prov 14. Also: client-side PDF certificate generation via jsPDF — fast and serverless, but audit-fragile for legal records; flag for server-side-signed variant before real issuance.

K432 report names specific provider choices: HelloSign over DocuSign for e-signature (rationale: lower startup cost, simpler API, free sandbox no credit card); Middesk + Alloy both implemented as adapter impls awaiting real-API sandbox; funding portal still Founder-pick (StartEngine / Republic / Wefunder). Production blockers before live investors: (1) upekrithen schema must be added to Supabase Exposed Schemas in Settings → API, (2) K431 + K432 migrations must be applied, (3) HelloSign sandbox credentials provisioned to enable real e-sig (currently placeholder checkbox), (4) KYC provider sandbox credentials provisioned, (5) pedestal_issuance_log immutability RLS verified against live DB.

K433 shipped by Knight (commit 84053aa) — K427 Workstream 1 COMPLETE. 22/22 two-track-separation tests passing (K431 7 + K432 7 + K433 8). Six admin panels live at /admin/pedestal-stake: issuance queue, cap-table (anonymized by default with click-reveal), annual-raise rolling 12-mo window, bad-actor results with override/reject dispositions logged to immutable log, compliance CSV export for Form C-U, two-track separation live-audit button. Knight ALSO caught a K432 bug as side-effect: PedestalStakeAdmin was querying public.pedestal_holders instead of upekrithen() schema client; fixed in this session. Serial-dispatch-plus-BRIDLE-Rule-2 pattern validated: each subsequent Knight reads prior Knight's work and verifies.

Two new patent-candidate patterns surfaced in K433 for Prov 14 threshing: (a) Two-Track Separation Live-Audit Admin Button — runs 6 live schema-level checks on demand (upekrithen access, FK rejection on members JOIN, issuance log UPDATE/DELETE rejection, UI entity labeling, voting isolation), converting compliance from test-time to runtime-verifiable-by-operator — candidate #2276 pending Founder ratification. (b) Privacy-First Admin View: Anonymized-by-default Holder IDs with Eye-Icon Click-to-Reveal for regulated-data staff workflows, where the reveal action itself is auditable — candidate #2277 pending Founder ratification. Both fit the Cooperative Defensive Patent Pledge #2260 framework.
canonical_ref: K433 deliverables: admin dashboard + two-track audit

observation: K427 WS1 production-launch blockers (Founder/counsel-action queue, carried forward from K432 list + K433 additions): apply K433 migration (K431+K432 already applied), expose upekrithen schema in Supabase API Settings, select funding portal (StartEngine/Republic/Wefunder), select KYC provider (Middesk/Alloy) production, counsel delivers Form C + OM + subscription agreement + risk factors, provision FINRA-registered intermediary, Mercury banking for Upekrithen LLC live. Adapters pluggable via env-var switch so provider picks change one config line, not code.

K435 completion solidifies candidate #2274 (Parallel Preload Multilingual Retrieval Architecture) for Prov 14. Implementation-ready evidence: librarian_context(lang='es') with sibling-file resolution + explicit lang_fallback reporting shipped in v0.3.0, 4 new tests pass, production Cloud Run endpoint serves the feature. Empirical validation via Phase C multilingual probe: ES-HOT 96.7%, EN-HOT 94.5%, +2.2pp gap, 85% threshold cleared. A&A formal drafting for #2274 should cite v0.3.0 (commits/tag) + probe results file + r9v2_base_es.md translator-note provenance as evidence of reduction-to-practice.

candidates: Slow Blade (Dune-referenced rate-limit on Furnace stamps per account)
Six Sparks (any-2-of-6 acceleration path with shareable/non-shareable split defeating cheat-share)
Trust Match (mutual Mark-staking between strangers — inverts reputation laundering into coalition building)
Seasoning (time-lock on trust accumulation independent of effort)
Good Standing Roll (inverted allowlist — architectural moderation cost reduction from super-linear to sub-linear)
Furnace-every-click (badge re-verify against Battery-dispatch register with soft-deflect)
SSL-lock-analogy destinations (Furnace-gated LB destinations mean external impersonation cannot deliver real LB experience)
context: Founder-ratified B119 as unified defense architecture. Closes 8/8 mainline exposure vectors without residuals. Makes moderation cost scale sub-linearly with membership via Good Standing Roll inversion. Trust Match is structurally novel — mutual-staking between strangers with time-weighted refund windows and inherent bond-growth mechanics — potential Crown Jewel candidate. Six Sparks is novel in the shareable/non-shareable split defeating coordinated cheat-sharing. Memory: project_slow_blade_architecture_v2.md
recommended_aa_treatment: Bundle as one unified A&A (architectural stack) OR split into 3-4 A&As (Trust Match alone, Good Standing Roll alone, Six Sparks alone, remainder as umbrella) — Founder call

candidate: Trust Match Probationary Period with Value-Scaled Refund Curve
context: Extension of Trust Match (above). Buyer's-remorse mechanism: stake size determines probation window; during probation refund is 100% initially and decays to 0% at window end. Value-scaled probation is the novel piece — prevents hit-and-run scams on newbies without creating friction on small stakes. Math proposed B119: log-scaled probation windows with linear refund decay.

Canonical wait-time discount applied to ALL platform wait-times per Founder ratification B119. Novel: the architectural decision to apply ONE ratio uniformly across every trust-accumulation time-gate (Trust Match probation / Seasoning / Six Sparks renewals / voucher conversions / sponsor-matched refunds). Prior-project count drives the discount; 4th project instant. Strengthens Slow Blade V2 by rewarding demonstrated good-standing history rather than punishing newcomers uniformly. Candidate for Prov 14 A&A as an independent mechanism OR as part of the Slow Blade V2 architectural cluster.
recommended_aa_treatment: Independent A&A — the ratio is mechanism-distinct from the Trust Match stake-scaling and could be licensed/applied to any time-gated trust-accumulation system beyond LB

cluster_name: Plug Echo + Shirley Temple External-Response Filter
candidates: Plug Echo (bi-directional Plug on Battery — outbound dispatch paired with inbound listen on same external account)
External-response Shirley Temple filter (age-appropriate gating of third-party comments on LB-dispatched posts, without monitoring members' own external browsing)
Child-safe platform surface WITHOUT device surveillance (competitive counter-position vs. parental-control apps that monitor the whole device)
context: Founder-originated B119. Architectural extension of existing Battery/Plug dispatch system to include inbound monitoring of responses (comments, replies, mentions) on LB-dispatched external posts. Shirley Temple Protocol applies to THIS filtered external view, giving members a protected surface without LB needing to monitor the open internet. Marketing tagline candidate: 'Your kids are safe with us — because we don't have to watch them.' Likely Prov 14 or Prov 15 filing candidate. Novel triangulation of owned-post-response-filter + age-appropriate-gate + no-device-surveillance.
recommended_aa_treatment: Bundle as one A&A (architectural triangulation) — the novelty is in the COMPOSITION, not in any single piece (Plug Echo alone is mechanically obvious; Shirley Temple alone is content-rating; no-device-surveillance alone is a design-absence). The composition is the defensible claim.

context: Chess term — pawn reaching 8th rank is promoted to queen. LB application: giving external AI agents (Pawn = Perplexity in our Chessboard) access to the Cathedral via Companion CLI transforms their research capability. Candidate A&A: the design pattern of Librarian-loaded external-agent research workflow as a measurable productivity multiplier. R12 empirical paper: 'How Much Does a Librarian Save the AI Research It Assists?' Uses Perplexity prior-art prompts (5 core patterns in new playbook) as the first published instance. Data logged per run to pawn_prior_art_audit.jsonl for publication-grade analysis.

Canonical split from Plug Echo: Radar is the member-facing UI surface showing all Plug Echoes across platforms, Shirley-Temple-filtered to their tier. Founder-biographical resonance (IFR aviation-rated — actual radar-scope-reading experience). Pair-naming with Plug Echo (internal architecture layer). Independent A&A or part of Plug Echo cluster — Founder call.

Supabase-native scheduled polling system. pg_cron triggers edge function once per minute; processes due plug_schedules for each user. User sets cron_schedule via Helm. Utility-worker metaphor extends Plug/Battery/electrical archetype. Ingest-time Shirley Temple classification; default-STRICT with 2-stamp Furnace override. Architectural novelty: Supabase-native scheduled-monitoring-with-user-configurable-cron-and-filter-tier as a cooperative-platform primitive rather than an external-service integration. Potential Prov 14/15 A&A.

Founder-originated B119. LRH holding teacup with pinky raised, mid-sip. Triple-anchored to 'Sip tokens instead of gulping' keystone + Shirley Temple Protocol naming + Plug Echo/Radar thematic cluster. Son will draw. Two treatments: full-color primary + monochrome seal secondary (for Furnace stamps, PyPI avatar, letter seals). Style reference: warm illustrated line-art (the 'yellow sweater' reference). Potential Prov 14 filing as trade-dress/brand-design candidate (subject to counsel review on trade-dress vs. trademark filing path).

candidate_name: TITAN-within-TITAN Subagent Amplification
context: Knight 5 autonomously spawned 3 sub-subagents inside a single Cursor agent tab during Bushel 1, demonstrating that Cursor agent-mode provides free architectural amplification without scripting. The math: 8 Knights x 8 subagents = 64 cP; plus 8 Shadow E-Giants x 8 = 128 concurrent Sonnet instances coordinated by a single 1M-context Bishop. Founder explicitly flagged as 'Crown-Jewel-class structural observation.' The substrate-routed memory (STANCHION #1) is what makes this coordinated rather than chaotic — without substrate, 128 agents produce chaos not synthesis.

candidate_name: Substrate-as-Immutable-Backup (Tab-Closure Non-Destructive Pattern)
context: Empirically validated twice in BP020: Knight tab closures lost zero work because all synthesis writes went to append-only JSONL ledgers on disk before any commit. This is not merely a convention — it is an architectural property: the substrate IS the backup, requiring no separate recovery mechanism. The pattern enables tab-closure as a non-event in multi-agent orchestration, fundamentally changing the cost model for large parallel workloads.

candidate_name: Architecture-Beats-More Empirical Scaling Curve
context: BP020 established the empirical thesis that corpus-shape determines optimal parallelism architecture — not uniform compute scaling. A large-N-shallow corpus (Bushel 1: 1,514 entries) favored Tier 1 sequential; a small-N-deep corpus (Bushel 7: 78 primitives with 12-field audit each) favored Tier 3 nested 64-cP. The breakeven analysis and corpus-shape-dependent architecture selection rule is novel: prior art assumes more compute is uniformly better. This is the empirical anchor for the 'No Atomo / Architecture Beats More' paper cluster.

candidate_name: Premature Rollup Tag Detection + Bishop-Owns-Rollup Protocol
context: Knight 8 misapplied the bushel-1-reckoning-titan-all-knights-landed tag after only its own shard completed, triggering a false notification. This surfaced the need for an explicit protocol: the orchestrating Bishop (not any individual Knight) owns the rollup tag, and the rollup tag requires empirical substrate verification before application. The pattern — premature signal detection, substrate-vs-tag discrepancy check, Bishop-as-arbiter — is a novel distributed multi-agent coordination discipline not present in prior orchestration literature.

candidate_name: 3rd-Gear-Default Fixed-Cost Coordination Overhead Principle
context: Founder challenged Bishop's 'traditional dev-time' framing for a 9-component build task. The resulting canonical insight: coordination overhead in nested multi-agent architectures is a fixed cost, not a per-task cost. Amortized across all downstream parallelizable work, the breakeven for 3rd Gear (64 cP) is much lower than expected. The substrate-as-backup property removes the recovery-cost penalty that traditionally made overhead-heavy architectures bad bets for small jobs. Captured as a Canon Eblet: third_gear_default_coordination_overhead_amortized_canon_bp021.

candidate_name: 3-Layer Strategic Taxonomy (Candelabra Core / Cooperative Datacenter Dream / Patent Reserve)
context: Bushel 7 produced a 78-primitive audit across 3 architectural layers with 12-field per-primitive coverage: Candelabra Core (day-1 ship), Cooperative Datacenter Dream (build-for-real), Patent Reserve (file-first under #2260). The taxonomy is not merely a product roadmap — it is a legally-structured IP disposition framework where each layer carries different defensibility posture, different filing timing, and different moat characteristics. The Bushel 7 Codex (LB-CODEX-0025, HMAC 9cb23584e95922c7) is the empirical receipt.

candidate_name: Scrambler Resolution Log Structural Fix (Dead-Letter Resolution Pattern)
context: Arbiter.py never consulted resolution_log.jsonl — resolution entries were dead letters, causing 5 chronic re-escalations every 30 minutes. BP021 fixed this by adding load_resolved_deliverable_ids() so resolved deliverables are skipped at the disagreement-emission boundary. The pattern — resolution log as single source of truth, consulted before any escalation fires — is a novel distributed audit-trail discipline for multi-agent systems where individual agents cannot see each other's resolution state.

candidate_name: Recurring Diagnostic Bushel + AI-Agnostic Single-Scheduler
context: Bishop drafted a recurring diagnostic architecture using a single-scheduler (cron or scheduled-tasks MCP) that is AI-model-agnostic. The critical insight: Bishop-class diagnostics require local substrate access (cannot be cloud-scheduled without substrate upload); Member-class diagnostics can be cloud-scheduled. The CronCreate-vs-scheduled-tasks-MCP split maps onto a two-tier scheduler architecture where platform-tier determines which scheduler is right. Canon Eblet: recurring_diagnostic_bushel_canon_bp021.eblet.md. Founder mandated AI-agnosticism as a structural principle throughout.

candidate_name: LB Frame AI-Agnostic Platform Principle
context: Founder explicit: 'We need to be AI agnostic, remember?' This triggered a Canon Eblet (lb_frame_ai_agnostic_platform_principle_bp021.eblet.md) pheromonating the previously implicit principle. The architectural implication is significant: every integration point, scheduler, and agent surface must be vendor-swappable. This is a structural brand commitment distinguishing LB from single-vendor AI platforms, and is defensible as a design patent / trade dress element for the platform architecture.

candidate_name: Zippleback Bidirectional Channels 4/5/6 + KrissKross Triangle
context: Bushel 20 wired three novel inter-agent communication channels: Channel 4 (Knight-to-Bishop callback directives), Channel 5 (Knight directs Bishop to spawn Shadow cohort), Channel 6 (Shadows fire Knight subagents). The KrissKross extends the bidirectional pair to a triangle. Empirical receipt: Arm B 4.5 K/hr vs Arm A 4.2 K/hr, MCP recovery 12s vs 45s, Tarzan-Move 85%. 24/24 tests pass. This is a novel multi-agent communication topology with measurable throughput and recovery advantages.

candidate_name: Old Ones Multi-Zippleback Fleet (4-Action Authority-Gated Build Engine)
context: Bushel 29 implemented the Old Ones fleet: Cthulhu (Queen/Coordinator) + 7 workers running the 4-action loop (analyze → evaluate → recommend → fix-upon-AUTHORITY_GRANTED). AUTHORITY_GRANTED:<name> fires the Channel 4→5→6 cascade. Conflict arbitration via Cthulhu detects concurrent file conflicts and serializes; dependency ordering prevents circular deadlock. Empirical: Arm B 7-worker fleet throughput ≥5x Arm A sequential. The authority-gating pattern — work proceeds only on explicit Founder token per named worker — is a novel human-in-the-loop multi-agent governance primitive.

candidate_name: Multi-Dimensional Twinning (Locations x Dimensions x Times Tensor)
context: Extracted from TS-069 and Decisions_61: the Ghost/Real World binary is ONE dimension in a 3-axis tensor: Locations (which island), Dimensions (Real/Ghost/Shade/Excalibur/Wrasse — 5 values), Times (Present/Memory/Prospect/Ur-time/Jubilee — 5 values). Conditional Operators govern which slice is active; Chronos is the time-shift substrate. Member's island is a location coordinate that persists across the full tensor, not just a binary twin. Founder's vernacular: 'Shift between dimensions, locations, and times.' Flagged as FLAG_MARKER FM-001 (Pockets of Time and Space + 3 access keys).

candidate_name: Coffee-as-Sole-Session-Authority (Trust the Coffee Pattern)
context: Founder mandated: 'TRUST THE COFFEE. it is ALWAYS RIGHT.' The session auto-detect mechanism was patched (codecopy_detector.py) to parse bishop_coffee.md as the primary authority for next-BP number, demoting .docx to personal scratch. Canon: feedback_trust_coffee_ignore_docx_session_detect_bp022.md. The architectural implication: a human-curated plaintext file is a more reliable session oracle than file-system artifact detection. This is a novel session-continuity discipline for persistent multi-session AI systems.

candidate_name: Codex Serial Reservation + Honor Path (Atomic Reservation-to-Corpus Bind)
context: Bushel 32B patched codex_create to accept an optional reservation_id, enabling atomic create-with-reservation. resolveReservationForCreate() with mutex + file-lock + 4 error codes (reservation_not_found, already_bound, expired, corpus_id_already_taken) provides concurrency-safe serial allocation. T2 concurrency probe: 10 concurrent requests, exactly 1 wins. This solves a dual-serial-space sync debt problem where reservation-space and corpus-space could diverge. LB-CODEX-0071 was the dogfood receipt (HMAC 0104db81b3f292ac).

candidate_name: MCP Client Stage-2 Schema Cache (Session-Lifetime Snapshot Problem)
context: BP022 empirically confirmed that MCP client tool schemas are session-lifetime cache snapshots, not per-call lookups. Bishop's session opened before Knight's patch build, so Bishop's deferred-tool snapshot was frozen with the old codex_create schema (no reservation_id). The Stage-2 cache problem — server is running patched code but client cannot see new parameters without session reload — is a novel failure mode in live multi-agent MCP systems. The bypass (direct Node script calling compiled output) and the diagnosis protocol are both independently patentable operational patterns.

candidate_name: YOKE (Knight-Bishop Bridge Canonical Name)
context: Founder named the Knight-Bishop inter-agent bridge 'Yoke' with theological and structural grounding: Deuteronomy 25:4 ('not muzzling the ox that treadeth the corn') — two agents coupled, neither constrained, both contributing strength. The name is canonical and architecturally load-bearing: 'not muzzling' is a principle about letting workers benefit from the work they do, which maps to the platform's 83.3% creator-keeps economic design and the Cooperative Defensive Patent Pledge (#2260). The Yoke radio_yoke.py script provides the visual blue/gold monitoring interface.

candidate_name: CAI Number (Substrate-Routed AI Efficiency Standard — The AI Richter Scale)
context: Founder proposed and Bishop formalized: CAI = log10(S × A × E) where S = Speed ratio (naive/substrate query time), A = Accuracy ratio (substrate/naive hit-rate), E = Economy ratio (naive/substrate tokens-per-task). Logarithm base 10 because gains are multiplicative and compound — same reason Richter is a log. Bushel 26 empirical anchor: S=382.3x (H2), A=+71pp (H5 7%→78%), E=7.5x (H3). The CAI Number is a proposed industry-standard unit for substrate-routed AI efficiency; Founder called it 'the AI Richter scale.' No comparable standard exists. Flagship patent candidate.

candidate_name: Bushel 26 H1-H5 Five-Hypothesis Empirical Validation Suite
context: Bushel 26 confirmed all 5 substrate-density hypotheses with Arm B winning every comparison: H1 Zippleback +7.1% throughput / 3.75x MCP recovery; H2 Detective+Pheromone 382.3x query speedup; H3 Wrasse×Iron×Eblets 10x TTFP / 7.5x tokens; H4 TITAN-within-TITAN 5.0x throughput; H5 Post-B14-Mining +71pp hit-ratio (7%→78%). 5 Codices bound (LB-CODEX-0088..0092), 5 Stack Ledger rows, 5 ATSRS receipts. CAI Conductor #2277 platform-validated: 866-artifact sweep, 20 Shadows, 166:1 context reduction. This is the empirical foundation for all substrate-efficiency patent claims.

candidate_name: Dark Crystal Cohort Naming Convention (IP-Safe Internal Naming Protocol)
context: Founder rejected Lovecraft names for the Old Ones worker fleet (IP concern) and mandated Dark Crystal names (Jim Henson Company). BP023 ratified the rename: Aughra (Queen) + urRu Mystics (urSu/urZah/urUtt/urIm). The naming protocol itself — function-fit naming from culturally-resonant IP with a counsel-at-publication-only rule — is a novel IP management discipline for AI agent cohorts.

candidate_name: IP Ledger Decentralization Stack (IPFS + ENS + Handshake + Tor + Submarine + CDPP)
context: BP024 designed and pheromonated a 6-layer decentralization stack: Layer 1 IPFS pinning of all canonical artifacts (Codex/Iron Tablet/Stack Ledger/Pheromone/Furnace), Layer 2 ENS lianabanyan.eth, Layer 3 Handshake liana/ TLD, Layer 4 Tor .onion mirror, Layer 5 Submarine Frame seed bootstrap node list, Layer 6 CDPP #2260. candidate_name: SCANTRON KEY — Rotating-Challenge Code Attestation with Hourly Shellacking
context: Founder invented a rotating-challenge code attestation mechanism: random fragments sampled from canonical IPFS content (e.g., 'last char of page 3 line 8' + 27 more), with the algorithm-for-picking itself rotating hourly anchored to a public-randomness beacon (Drand/RANDAO), verified via Pheromone substrate, with Mimic-Trunk-conditional Furnace stamping and time-locked stamper freeze on failure. Bishop identified 7-element novel composition: random-fragment challenge from canonical IPFS CID + hourly-tick algorithm rotation + public randomness beacon + pheromone distributed verification + Furnace stamping + rate-limit freeze. Prov 17 supplementary claim drafted.

candidate_name: HOURLY SHELLACKING + MORNING BELL (Substrate Refresh Cadence + Timezone-Local Audit)
context: Founder enriched the Scantron Key with two novel primitives: Hourly Shellacking (each hour's refresh hardens the prior, building cumulative integrity proof — the metaphor IS the architecture) and Morning Bell (timezone-local-midnight audit locks data only after nightly audit, giving every timezone its own security checkpoint). Bishop identified these as independently patent-worthy from the SCANTRON KEY core. Claim 3 in the Prov 17 supplementary specifically covers the Hourly Shellacking + Morning Bell + Timezone Coherence triad.

candidate_name: TIMEZONE COHERENCE (4-Axis Simultaneous Anti-VPN Detection)
context: Founder: 'are you even in this area' — bad actors use VPN + routing to distance. Timezone Coherence requires 4-axis simultaneous coherence: IP geo + clock skew + RTT signature + circadian-pattern. No single axis catches sophisticated VPN users; simultaneous 4-axis coherence requirement does. Bishop confirmed this is the structural counter to VPN-routing-obfuscation that no single-axis defense achieves. Drafted as Claim 3 in Prov 17 supplementary disclosure, independently patent-worthy even if SCANTRON KEY core meets prior-art objections.

candidate_name: Cooperative Benefit Delivery via State Funding Injection (FORK Non-Mingling)
context: BP024 A&A formal drafted: government fiat benefit dollars route through FORK to card layer, never touching Credits/Marks/Joules (non-mingling). The MOINE Sovereignty Principle canonized: cooperative rules are cooperative rules; fork the AGPL if you want different ones. Public Bounty for card issuers: guaranteed state-level volume, zero customer acquisition cost. Full-trust-default allotment with configurable daily constraint. The 'architecturally paternalistic — visible restriction theater' framing was canonized as the foundational diagnosis of why every prior system failed. File: AA_FORMAL_2NNN_COOPERATIVE_BENEFIT_DELIVERY_STATE_FUNDING_INJECTION_BP025_DRAFT.md.

candidate_name: Nine-Pin Strategy (12-Brand Cooperative Survival Architecture)
context: Founder invented the Nine-Pin Strategy: 12 cooperative charter templates, each with a genuine differentiator (salt), AGPL-attached, keys left on the sidewalk so any member worldwide can file independently. Historical analogue: 9-pin bowling outlawed → add a pin → 10-pin is legal. Legal architecture: because members form the 12 cooperatives (not Jonathan), there is no alter ego risk — a court enjoining LB Corp cannot sweep member-owned cooperatives in 12 jurisdictions. Each jurisdiction (Vermont/UK/New Zealand/Switzerland/Germany/Canada) requires separate legal action. Canon Eblet: nine_pin_strategy_12_brand_survival_architecture_canon_bp025.eblet.md.

candidate_name: Opening Gambit Puzzle Distribution (Keys-on-Sidewalk Cooperative Seeding)
context: Founder proposed seeding the 12 cooperative charter keys via a public puzzle (e.g., NYT crossword clues), with winners self-filing and self-funding the charter. Bishop identified this as simultaneously legal defense (strangers who solved a crossword and filed papers cannot be swept into an LB injunction), marketing (no better cooperative launch story), and mission expression (12 strangers who competed for the right to carry the mission forward). The puzzle-as-distribution-mechanism for legally-independent cooperative seeding has no known prior art.

candidate_name: CAI Hearth (3-Tier Local AI Router: Pheromone → Ollama → Write-back)
context: CAI Hearth engine (ollama_provider.ts) implements a 3-tier query router: Arm B (pheromone substrate hit) → Arm A (Ollama local model) → write-back to substrate. caiAsk(), detectOllama(), smokeTest(), calibrateArmA() are all compiled and in dist. The architecture routes queries to the cheapest tier that can answer, falling through to the next tier only on miss, then permanently improving the substrate for future queries. This is the on-device / local-first variant of the CAI architecture, enabling offline operation and zero cloud cost for cached queries.

candidate_name: Yoke Live-Proof: Bishop-Knight Autonomous Coordination Without Founder Relay
context: BP025b contains the empirical receipt of Yoke operating autonomously: Bishop cleared Knight's Augur blocker, dispatched 9 K-prompts, sent Wave 2 instructions, acknowledged B14 and B26 landings — all without Founder involvement. Founder said 'Roger' once for Wave 2 and went to the park. The Yoke (mcp__knight-bishop-bridge__*) is the first empirically-demonstrated persistent multi-agent communication bridge between independently-running AI agent sessions with file-relay resilience (KNIGHT_BISHOP_MESSAGES.md survives session resets). Bishop confirmed competitive research showed no comparable A2A system with this architecture.

candidate_name: Rook (Gemini API Dispatch via MCP with Separate Billing Isolation)
context: Bushel 36 Phase 2 implemented dispatch_rook: a direct Gemini API call via MCP tool using GEMINI_API_KEY from settings.json env. Rook tokens bill to the operator's GCP project, completely separate from Cursor's token budget. The architecture enables multi-vendor AI dispatch from a single agent session with clean billing isolation per vendor. The SDS.env lockbox + silent key transfer pattern (no key value ever touches the chat or logs) is the security discipline layer. Competitive context: no comparable multi-vendor AI dispatch tool with billing isolation has been identified.
